architecture.summary: LLM-MCTS integrates an LLM-induced commonsense world model with Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) for large-scale task planning. The LLM generates an initial belief distribution over object locations by sampling position predictions, which MCTS uses to construct and explore a search tree over partially observable states. During tree search, the LLM also serves as a heuristic policy (via PUCT), guiding action selection to promising branches. Observations update the belief, and the system returns actions with highest estimated value after simulation.

biblio.title: Large Language Models as Commonsense Knowledge for Large-Scale Task Planning

claims.statement: LLM-MCTS outperforms both MCTS alone and policies induced by LLMs (GPT2 and GPT3.5) by a wide margin for complex, novel tasks
GPT3.5-MCTS achieves 91.4% success rate on simple seen tasks in VirtualHome
GPT3.5-MCTS achieves 72.6% success rate on compositional seen tasks
Ablation: LLM-MCTS with uniform state prior achieves only 3.2% success on simple seen tasks, versus 91.4% with LLM-generated belief
Ablation: LLM-MCTS without heuristic policy achieves 0.0% success across all task categories
If the description length of the world model is substantially smaller than that of the policy, using LLM as a world model for model-based planning is likely better than using LLM solely as a policy

claims.dataset: Kaggle World cities database, 68 large cities + 62 mid-size cities, 800 city pairs
claims.statement: For air travel planning, LLM model + search algorithm consistently outperforms LLM policy
UCT (MCTS without commonsense knowledge) achieves 0.0% success rate across all task categories
